county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Orange County formed 1852 from Jefferson. County seat is Orange. East Texas / Piney Woods region with timber industry and plantation heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Orange. Southeast Texas county (Sabine River border with Louisiana). Heritage House Museum. Stark Museum of Art. Orange was a major lumber shipping port. Significant Cajun heritage (cross-border with Louisiana). Check Jefferson for pre-1852 records. Petrochemical industry records (20th century).
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Orange County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1852; County Clerk in Orange holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Orange County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1852 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Orange County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1852. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Orange County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1852. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Orange County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1852 to 1950 for Orange County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Orange County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Orange County from 1852. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Palo Pinto County formed 1856 from Bosque, Navarro. County seat is Palo Pinto. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Palo Pinto. Central-west Texas county. Palo Pinto County Historical Association. Possum Kingdom Lake area. Check Bosque and Navarro for pre-1856 records. Frontier settlement and ranching heritage.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Palo Pinto County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1856; County Clerk in Palo Pinto holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Palo Pinto County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1856 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Palo Pinto County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1856. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Palo Pinto County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1856. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Palo Pinto County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1856 to 1950 for Palo Pinto County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Native American Records -- Palo Pinto County Area (various tribes)
source_profiles.repository: NARA, Fort Worth / Oklahoma Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-settlement and frontier-era records for various tribes in the Palo Pinto County area. Includes Indian Affairs records, military campaign records, and reservation records at NARA Fort Worth. Texas frontier counties experienced significant Native American conflict through the 1870s.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Palo Pinto County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Palo Pinto County from 1856. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Panola County formed 1846 from Harrison, Shelby. County seat is Carthage. East Texas / Piney Woods region with timber industry and plantation heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Carthage. East Texas county. Panola County Heritage Museum. Tex Ritter heritage. Check Harrison and Shelby for pre-1846 records. Significant African American community. Oil and gas production.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Panola County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1846; County Clerk in Carthage holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Panola County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Panola County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Estate inventories listing enslaved persons, bills of sale, tax rolls listing slaveholders and enslaved persons counts, and Freedmen's Bureau records (NARA). Panola County was part of the Texas plantation economy.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Parker County formed 1855 from Bosque, Navarro. County seat is Weatherford. North Texas / Blackland Prairie region with farming and urban growth heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Weatherford. North Texas county. Chandor Gardens. Parker County Heritage Society. Oliver Loving (cattle drive pioneer) heritage. Check Bosque and Navarro for pre-1855 records. Parker County was a frontier defense post.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Parmer County formed 1876 from Bexar. County seat is Farwell. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Pecos County formed 1871 from Presidio. County seat is Fort Stockton. West Texas / Permian Basin region with ranching, oil, and frontier heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Fort Stockton. Trans-Pecos county. Annie Riggs Museum. Fort Stockton (frontier military post). Comanche Springs heritage. Check Presidio for pre-1871 records. Ranching and oil heritage.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Livingston. East Texas county. Heritage Museum of Livingston. Alabama-Coushatta Indian Reservation — tribal records essential for Native American research. Check Liberty for pre-1846 records. Timber heritage.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Potter County formed 1876 from Bexar. County seat is Amarillo. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Amarillo. Panhandle county organized 1887. Panhandle-Plains Historical Museum in Canyon (Randall County) is the major regional repository serving Potter County. Amarillo was major cattle shipping point. Amarillo Public Library Southwest Collection. Check Bexar for pre-organization records. Helium industry heritage.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Potter County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1876; County Clerk in Amarillo holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Presidio County formed 1850 from Bexar. County seat is Marfa. West Texas / Permian Basin region with ranching, oil, and frontier heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Marfa. Trans-Pecos county — one of the oldest settled areas in Texas (Spanish presidio 1683). Marfa and Presidio County Museum. Fort Leaton State Historic Site. Border area with significant Mexican heritage. Check Bexar for pre-1850 records. Chinati Foundation art heritage. Mexican consulate records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Presidio County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1850; County Clerk in Marfa holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Presidio County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Presidio County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Spanish Colonial / Mission Records -- Presidio County Area
source_profiles.repository: Catholic Archives of San Antonio / TSLAC / Bexar Archives at UT-Austin
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Spanish colonial records including mission registers (baptisms, marriages, burials), presidio records, and land grants. Presidio County area was part of the Spanish colonial system. Records at Catholic Archives of San Antonio, TSLAC, and Bexar Archives at UT-Austin.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Colonial-era records vary in survival; many originals at Catholic Archives or in Mexican archives
